title: Respondent Information

questions.prompt: Name of Artisan
Craft
State
District/Village
Gender
Age
Educational Qualification
Contact Number
Years of Experience
Family Members Involved
Date of Interview
Interviewer Name

title: ORIGIN, HISTORY, PLACE AND PERSONAL JOURNEY

questions.prompt: Can you tell us about the history and origin of your craft tradition?
How did you first learn this craft and who taught you? Share your journey into this craft.
How long has your family or community been associated with this craft?
Are there any stories, legends, myths, or local memories associated with this craft?
How has the place where you live influenced your craft, materials, designs, or techniques?
Have there been any major changes in the craft over your lifetime?
What traditional knowledge has been lost or is disappearing?
What makes your craft unique compared to similar crafts in other regions?
What part of the craft's history should younger generations know? What may inspire them?
What does this craft mean to you personally?

title: FAMILY, COMMUNITY, GENDER AND KNOWLEDGE TRANSFER

questions.prompt: Is this mainly a family craft, a community craft, or both? (Whole village, or scattered over different villages)
Who usually learns the craft first in the family? (gender issues can be raised)
How is work usually distributed among family members?
What roles do men and women typically perform in the craft?
How do children and younger family members learn the craft?
Are young people interested in continuing this profession? Why or why not?
How important is family involvement for the survival of the craft?
Do artisans in your community help each other or work independently?
How does formal education affect the possibility of younger generations taking up the craft? (do educated children continue?)
Do women and men receive equal recognition for their work in this craft?
What challenges do women artisans face?
What support would encourage future generations to continue this craft?
Is it becoming easier or more difficult to continue this tradition within the family?
What makes it harder for young people to continue in this craft today? What can improve to motivate them?

title: MIGRATION, MOBILITY AND CHANGING LIVELIHOODS

questions.prompt: Has your family always lived in this area or have they migrated?
Have artisans migrated to other places for work or better opportunities?
How has migration affected the continuity of the craft?
Has migration changed access to raw materials or markets?
Do young artisans leave the village for other jobs?
What occupations attract young people away from the craft?
Have returning migrants introduced new ideas or techniques?
Has urbanization affected the craft community?
How has mobility changed the way products are sold?
How do you think migration will affect the future of the craft?

title: RAW MATERIALS, PROCUREMENT AND RESOURCE SECURITY

questions.prompt: What are the main raw materials used in your craft?
How do you identify good-quality raw materials?
Where do you obtain your raw materials?
Has the availability of raw materials changed over time?
Price of Raw Material? Have prices of raw materials increased significantly?
Are there seasonal variations in availability?
How are raw materials processed before use?
What challenges do you face in obtaining good-quality materials?
How does raw material quality affect the final product?
What measures are needed to ensure long-term raw material availability?

title: PROCESS OF MAKING AND WORKFLOW

questions.prompt: Please explain the complete production process from raw material selection to finished product.
What are the major stages involved in the process?
Which stage requires the highest level of skill and why?
Which stage takes the most time?
Which stage contributes most to product quality?
Which stage causes the highest material wastage?
What are the most common mistakes made during production?
How do experienced artisans identify and correct these mistakes?
Which stages are physically most demanding?
Which stages are most difficult for beginners to learn?
Which stages have changed over the years and why?
Which stages should always remain manual and why?
Which stages could be mechanized without affecting authenticity?
Are there any scientific principles, calculations, measurements, or traditional rules followed during production?
If you could improve one stage of production, which stage would it be and how would you improve it?

title: TOOLS, EQUIPMENT, DCH/STATE SUPPORT,ERGONOMICS AND TECHNOLOGICAL IMPROVEMENT

questions.prompt: What are the major tools used in your craft?
Which tools are traditional and which are modern?
Which tools are most essential for maintaining product quality?
Which tools require the highest skill to operate?
Which tools are used most frequently during production?
Which tools cause the greatest physical strain or discomfort?
Do artisans experience pain in the hands, wrists, shoulders, back, neck, knees, or eyes due to prolonged tool use?
Which tools are considered unsafe or prone to causing injuries?
How are tools maintained, sharpened, or repaired?
Have tool designs changed over time?
If existing tools could be redesigned, what changes would make them easier, safer, faster, or more comfortable to use?
Are there any tools that you wish existed but are currently unavailable?
Which tasks consume the most time because of tool limitations?
How can technology improve productivity while preserving craftsmanship?
If engineers were to develop improved tools for your craft, what features should they prioritize?
Have you received any support from the Development Commissioner (Handicrafts), the State, or other agencies regarding tools, training, or technology?

title: SCIENCE, INDIGENOUS KNOWLEDGE AND TECHNOLOGY OF THE CRAFT

questions.prompt: What traditional knowledge is essential for practicing this craft successfully?
How do artisans determine the quality of raw materials?
Are there specific measurements, proportions, or calculations followed?
How do artisans judge moisture, strength, flexibility, hardness, or suitability of materials?
What traditional techniques improve durability and longevity?
Are there any weather-related considerations during production?
How do temperature, humidity, or seasonal conditions affect the craft?
What scientific principles do you think unknowingly operate within the craft?
How do artisans solve technical problems during production?
Have modern technologies influenced the craft?
Which traditional practices are scientifically effective?
Are there any local innovations developed by artisans themselves?
What technical knowledge is at risk of disappearing?
What aspects should researchers document before they are lost?
How can scientific research support the future of this craft?

title: ECONOMICS: MARKET, SALES, PROFIT, THREATS, AND CHALLENGES:INCOME AND LIVELIHOOD

questions.prompt: What are your major sources of income?
How much does the craft contribute to household income?
What are the main ways through which you sell your work?
Do you mostly sell locally, through exhibitions, middlemen, government fairs, private buyers, or online?
What kind of products sell the most, and why?
How do you decide the price of a piece?
What are the biggest costs involved in your work today?
Where do you lose the most value — raw material, labour, transport, middlemen, delayed payments, or market access?
Has the market for your craft changed in recent years? In what ways?
What are the major costs involved in production?
Which products are most profitable?
How stable is income throughout the year?
Which season provides the highest sales?
Have earnings improved or declined over time?
What financial challenges do artisans face?
What economic changes would most improve artisan livelihoods?
Do women artisans face any different economic challenges in this craft?
What kinds of support would improve income the most: better tools, design inputs, direct market access, online sales, training, credit, or raw material support?

title: MARKETING, VALUE CHAINS AND DIGITAL COMMERCE

questions.prompt: How do customers usually learn about your products?
What are the main channels through which products are sold?
What role do middlemen play in marketing?
What advantages do middlemen provide?
What disadvantages do middlemen create?
How much control do artisans have over product pricing?
What factors influence the selling price?
Which products are easiest to sell?
Which products have declining demand?
Have customer preferences changed over time?
Do you use social media, WhatsApp, websites, or e-commerce platforms?
What challenges do artisans face while selling online?
How can digital marketing improve artisan incomes?
If artisans could sell directly to consumers without middlemen, how would that affect the craft?
What marketing support would most improve sales and visibility?

title: DIGITAL TECHNOLOGY, AI AND FUTURE PLATFORMS

questions.prompt: What kinds of phones or digital devices do you use in your daily life and work?
How comfortable are you with using smartphones and the internet?
What do you usually use your phone for in relation to your craft?
Do you search for materials, markets, videos, or designs online? If yes, how?
Have you ever used government portals, registration systems, or online applications related to your craft?
If not, what prevents you from using them more — language, confidence, poor internet, lack of time, or complexity?
Which language do you prefer when using a phone or digital platform?
Do you prefer reading, watching videos, listening to audio, or using voice-based search?
Would local-language, video-based, or voice-based support make digital systems easier for you?
What kind of digital help would be easiest and most useful for you — sales, design reference, training, applications, complaints, or communication?
Do younger family members help with digital work related to the craft? In what ways?
Would online training programs help artisans?
How can AI support artisans without replacing creativity?
What concerns do you have regarding AI and automation?
What features should an ideal artisan portal include?
What should technology never replace in your craft?

title: SUSTAINABILITY, ENVIRONMENT, POLLUTION AND WASTE MANAGEMENT

questions.prompt: What types of waste are generated during production?
How is waste currently managed?
Can waste materials be reused or recycled?
Are there traditional methods of waste utilization?
Does production create smoke, dust, or pollution?
How are these environmental impacts managed?
Has climate change affected production or raw materials?
Are artisans concerned about environmental sustainability?
What sustainable practices are already followed?
What environmental improvements would help without affecting tradition?

title: RITUALS, BELIEFS AND INTANGIBLE HERITAGE

questions.prompt: Are there rituals performed before beginning work?
Do artisans worship any deity associated with the craft?
Are there auspicious days for starting production?
Are there festivals connected with the craft?
Do designs have religious or cultural meanings?
Are there taboos associated with the craft?
Are prayers offered before major orders or exhibitions?
How are traditions passed to younger generations?
Have any rituals disappeared over time?
How important are these beliefs to the identity of the craft?

title: HEALTH, OCCUPATIONAL HAZARDS AND REASONS FOR LEAVING THE CRAFT

questions.prompt: What health problems are commonly faced by artisans?
Which activities cause the most physical strain?
Do you experience pain, injuries, or long-term health issues?
Are protective measures used during work?
How does age affect your ability to continue the craft?
Why are young people leaving the profession?
Is income a major reason for leaving?
Are health-related issues forcing artisans to quit?
What improvements would encourage artisans to continue?
What can government and society do to prevent decline of the craft?

title: TRAINING, SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND FUTURE OF THE CRAFT

questions.prompt: How are new artisans trained?
How long does it take to become skilled?
Which skills are most difficult to learn?
Are formal training programs available?
What kind of training is currently missing?
Would digital learning resources help?
What is your vision for the future of this craft?

title: STORYTELLING, VISITOR INTERACTION AND COMMUNICATION CHALLENGES

questions.prompt: How do you explain your craft to visitors?
What questions do visitors ask most often?
Do language barriers affect communication with visitors?
Have you interacted with foreign tourists or buyers?
What difficulties do you face while explaining your craft to them?
Do visitors understand the labour and skill involved?
Are there stories behind your products that customers often miss?
Do you feel products are undervalued because their stories are not known?
Would brochures, videos, QR codes, or audio guides help communicate the craft?
What story about your craft would you most like every visitor to hear?
Would multilingual audio guides help communicate your knowledge?
Would QR-code-based storytelling improve customer engagement?
How can artisans better communicate the cultural value of their products to international audiences?

title: DESIGNS, MOTIFS, INNOVATION, RECOGNITION

questions.prompt: What are the main motifs, patterns, or design types used in your craft?
How do you learn and remember these motifs?
Approximately how many motifs, forms, or recurring design types do you use regularly?
How do new motifs or new designs emerge in your work?
What does innovation mean to you within your craft tradition?
How do you balance tradition and new ideas?
What kinds of new designs do customers ask for?
When does innovation feel useful, and when does it feel harmful to tradition?
Would you like support in developing new products or motifs while preserving the identity of the craft?
What kinds of innovations should come from artisans themselves rather than outsiders?
Have buyers, markets, exhibitions, or designers influenced your designs? In what ways?
What kinds of work or qualities do you think lead to State or National recognition?
In your view, what makes one artisan’s work stand out from another’s?
You have design training with designers. How do they help you? Do they bring in designs that are very different from the tradition?

title: PORTAL DEVELOPMENT, AI-ENABLED INPUTS, INTERFACES, NETWORKING, AND GRIEVANCES

questions.prompt: If there were a good online platform made for artisans, what should it ideally help you do first?
Would you find it useful to have a place to showcase your work and tell the story behind it?
What kind of design inspiration or reference support would help you most?
Would it be useful to connect with artisans from other regions through such a platform? Why?
What kind of communication with DCH or other agencies would you like to make easier online?
Would a system for complaints, suggestions, requests, or feedback be useful? What should it include?
What kind of information about tools, materials, markets, or training should such a platform provide?
If a platform could give ideas without replacing your creativity, what kind of AI-based support would you welcome?
Would image-based search, voice input, translation, local-language interfaces, or simple design suggestions be helpful?
Would you use a platform that helps you connect to markets, buyers, exhibitions, and training opportunities?
What should such a system never do, so that the dignity, originality, and ownership of the craft remain protected?
Are there any fears or concerns you would have about AI or digital systems entering craft practice?

title: ONLINE DISSEMINATION, STORYTELLING, CHILDREN, RESILIENCE, AND SURVIVAL OF THE CRAFT

questions.prompt: What stories about your craft do you think should be shared with the wider public?
Are there moments from your life or work that could inspire children and young people?
What kinds of simple videos, stories, or demonstrations would help others understand the value of your craft?
What struggles have you faced in continuing this work?
What has helped you persist despite difficulties?
Are there stories of success, resilience, recognition, or community support that should be recorded and shared?
What message would you like to give to the younger generation about craft and handwork?
What kinds of stories would make children and young people respect this craft more?
What do you think needs to happen for this craft to survive and flourish in the future?
What one support system is most needed for the survival of this craft?
If the whole country were to hear one thing from you about your craft, what would you want to say?

title: THE SCIENCE BEHIND THE CRAFT: PROBING QUESTIONS

questions.prompt: How do you know that a particular wood, bamboo, or other material is right for this work? What signs do you look for?
What differences do you notice between one material and another in terms of strength, softness, grain, flexibility, weight, or durability?
How do weather, moisture, humidity, or temperature affect the material and the making process?
How do you know when the material is properly dried, seasoned, or ready to use?
What happens if the material is too wet, too dry, too old, or of poor quality?
Which stages of the process require the most precision, and why?
How do you judge thickness, balance, proportion, fit, or symmetry while making an object?
What makes a finished piece strong, durable, smooth, or long-lasting?
What are the most common reasons why a piece cracks, bends, warps, loosens, or fails over time?
How do different tools affect the material differently during the process?
Are there any sounds, touch sensations, colours, smells, or visual signs that tell you the material is behaving properly or improperly?
Are there areas where scientific testing or technical support could help improve the craft without harming tradition?

title: GI TAG, RECOGNITION, CERTIFICATION AND AUTHENTICITY

questions.prompt: Is your craft covered under a Geographical Indication (GI) tag?
What benefits has the GI tag provided to artisans?
Are artisans aware of the GI registration and its importance?
Has the GI tag increased market value or recognition?
What challenges exist in utilizing GI benefits?
How can GI protection help preserve traditional knowledge?
Are counterfeit or imitation products affecting your market?
What additional certification or branding support would help artisans?

title: AWARDS, RECOGNITION AND INSTITUTIONAL SUPPORT

questions.prompt: Have you received any awards, honors, or recognition for your work?
What was the significance of those recognitions?
Are there artisans in your community who have received national or state awards?
What role do government institutions play in supporting artisans?
What support have you received from NGOs, universities, or research institutions?
What forms of recognition are most meaningful to artisans?
What additional support would help deserving artisans gain visibility?
How can institutions better promote traditional crafts nationally and internationally?
